description_long: This is a large, roughly cubic stone block with a weathered, gray surface and a slightly irregular texture, suggesting it is made from concrete or a similar material. The top face features a recessed rectangular area with a prominent metal handle or loop embedded at its center, likely for lifting or moving the block. One side of the block displays a geometric cutout with a series of black, stepped triangular shapes, possibly for drainage, ventilation, or decorative purposes. Another side has a solid black rectangular inset, which could be a vent, access panel, or simply a design element. The remaining faces are mostly plain, showing only the natural pitting and roughness of the stone. The block's substantial size and robust construction indicate it is intended as a heavy-duty urban barrier or obstacle.
description: A large, weathered gray stone block with a metal handle on top, geometric cutouts, and a rugged, industrial appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows a plain, rough gray stone face with subtle surface texture and the top handle just visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the top handle and a slanted edge on the recessed area, with no major cutouts visible.
description_view_2: This view highlights a stepped, triangular black cutout on the side, adding a distinctive geometric feature.
description_view_3: This side features a solid black rectangular inset, contrasting with the otherwise plain stone surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street as a vehicle barrier
attribute_installation_method: set directly on pavement or embedded in concrete
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows moderate weathering with pitting and discoloration, indicating exposure to the elements and urban pollution.
